title: Turn conflict rows into a frontend conflict drawer
summary: Reworked conflict visibility into a drawer-style inspection flow so overlapping TSX and JSX work can be reviewed with rationale, branches, and suggested tests in one place.
goal: Move conflict handling from a passive list to a dedicated frontend surface that explains why sessions overlap and what the operator should inspect next.
changesMade: Deepened the conflict panel to show session titles and branch context together
Aligned conflict UI with the inspection card language from the session pane
Shaped conflict data so file overlaps and rationale labels stay grouped
Prepared the shell for future action buttons like compare and assign review

features.name: conflict drawer presentation
features.why: A high-signal review surface helps frontend teams inspect overlapping work before it turns into broken UX.

features.name: rationale-first conflict summaries
features.why: The operator needs to know why two sessions touched the same file, not just that they did.

features.name: shared conflict contract
features.why: Keep API and UI aligned as conflict payloads become richer.

decisions.decision: Treat conflicts as an inspection workflow instead of a warning badge
decisions.why: Frontend overlap often needs a human-quality judgment rather than a single binary signal.
The team needs reliable visibility before wiring any mutation or assignment actions into the UI.

openQuestions: Should the first action from a conflict drawer be compare context, open file, or assign reviewer?
resumePrompt: Continue the conflict drawer by reviewing dashboard/src/components/dashboard.tsx and then enrich dashboard/src/lib/data.ts with any missing rationale fields.
